title: Cloudflare Inc (NET) Q3 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Strong Revenue Growth Amid Leadership Changes
text: Cloudflare delivered strong quarterly results with revenue reaching $562 million, representing a 30.7% year-over-year increase. The company achieved an operating profit of $85.9 million with a 15.3% operating margin, while generating $75 million in free cash flow. The company reported a 23% increase in large customers, with revenue contribution from these customers growing 42% year-over-year. Dollar-based net retention rate improved to 119%, up 5 percentage points sequentially, reflecting strong platform adoption among existing clients. A significant development was the departure of executive CJ Desai, who is leaving to become CEO at another technology company. This leadership change could affect the company's continuity during a period of strategic transformation. Management emphasized the company's evolution from a product-led growth model to an enterprise sales approach. CEO Matthew Prince highlighted that the transformation from a product-led growth company to a true enterprise sales company is driving success, noting improvements in sales productivity and deal sizes. Despite growth achievements, the company faces headwinds including declining gross margins down 100 basis points sequentially and 350 basis points annually. Additionally, increased competition from hyperscalers in AI inference workloads presents a potential market share risk.
